title: First pass rootless tutorial
body:
First pass of the rootless tutorial.  This may be come the basis
for a User Guide of the same.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ugh, love the whitespace checks.  Will clean them up tomorrow, happy to take comments prior.
I think it is always easier to do the first pass at something like this in google docs.

Also someone should figure out how one would go about setting up and installing podman without root privilege.  IE No admin capabilities at all.
Could I build podman, slirp4netns, fuse-overlayfs and install them in ~/bin, and then get storage.conf and libpod.conf to use them and get them working in my homedir.

Moved to a Google Doc for further edits/comments at:  https://docs.google.com/document/d/1X4XOFTmKVtDQSNxq9NOBzwIPY3QrPpoCs4qmUBoLohM/edit.  If you'd like access, please send me an email and I'll share the doc with you.  Once the edit/reviews are completed in the document, I will move it here for a final review.
@TomSweeneyRedHat what's the state on this one?
I've a number of good comments in the doc file that I've got to polish and work with.  Hope to get to it early next week as I'll no longer by the bug czar.
I've repushed with the latest comments from the doc reviews included.  I think this is good enough now to merge and then pick at nits afterward as appropriate.
